areas.name: Park Slope
areas.reason: Brooklyn's stroller capital — gorgeous brownstones, great food-co-op energy, and a social scene built around couples and school calendars. At 37 and single you'd love the farmers market and feel out of place at the bar by 10pm.

areas.name: Murray Hill & Kips Bay
areas.reason: Walkable to the office and full of post-college energy — the bar scene runs five to ten years younger than you. Decent value for Manhattan, wrong decade.

areas.name: Financial District
areas.reason: Doorman-building value and a quick train to 34th St, but the neighborhood empties out after 7pm and on weekends. For a one-year NYC chapter, living where the city goes quiet defeats the point.

areas.name: Washington Heights
areas.reason: Real rent relief and the A express is no joke, but it's a 35–50 minute door-to-door and the nightlife is local-neighborhood, not destination. The savings buy you distance from everything you moved for.

areas.name: Sunnyside
areas.reason: Lovely, leafy 7-train value with great Irish pubs and Romanian bakeries — and quiet by 10. You'd be commuting to your social life every weekend.

areas.name: Upper West Side
areas.reason: Handsome, park-flanked, and convenient — and settled. The UWS is where people move after they've made their NYC memories, not to make them.

areas.name: SoHo & Nolita
areas.reason: The scene, the looks, the restaurants — and 1BR asking rents that blow past $5.5K before you've seen a lease. The stretch zone doesn't stretch this far.

areas.name: DUMBO & Downtown Brooklyn
areas.reason: Postcard bridge views and new-tower amenities, but tiny 1BR inventory at premium prices and corporate-quiet evenings. You'd live in a photograph, not a neighborhood.
